lines: salt spray stings your eyes as the merrow surfaces
webbed claws rake across the slick wet stone
a tide pool hides a pair of watching yellow eyes
you plant your feet before the wave can knock you down
the merrow drags a net of kelp behind its back
cold water floods the cave floor twice a day
gulls scream above the wrecked and rusted hull
you swing your blade before the tide rushes in
a hook shaped claw snags the edge of your shield
green scales flash beneath the churning surf
the sea cave floods and the exit starts to shrink
you brace against the rock as spray soaks your shirt
a webbed hand grips the ledge just above the waterline
the merrow hums a low and awful tune underwater
kelp tangles around your ankles in the shallows
you dodge a lunge and answer with a quick jab
barnacles cut your palm as you climb the wet rock
the wreck creaks as the tide pulls it further out
a shard of driftwood makes a clumsy but useful club
salt cracks on your knuckles after every swing
the cave mouth narrows as the water keeps rising
a fin breaks the surface then vanishes again
you steady your footing on the slick tide worn stone
the merrow shrieks and slides back into the foam
spray stings your face but you hold your ground
a rusted anchor chain rattles against the hull
you hear splashing before you see the claws
the tide pool ripples with something large beneath
a crab scuttles past as the fight begins
you jab twice and the merrow finally flinches
cold spray fills the cave with a briny mist
the monster circles slow along the shallow edge
you grip the hilt tighter as the wave rolls in
webbed feet slap against the wet stone floor
the merrow lunges and you barely twist away
seaweed drifts past as blood mixes with the foam
you climb over the wreck to reach higher ground
a bell somewhere below rings out through the cave
the tide keeps rising and the fight keeps going
you feint left then strike hard on the right
claws rake your shield and leave three long grooves
the merrow retreats toward the deeper water
you press forward before it can slip away again
spray and foam hide the creature until it strikes
a wave crashes and nearly sweeps you off the rock
you steady your breath and raise your blade again
the merrow circles back for one more desperate lunge
kelp and driftwood churn in the rising water
you finish the fight as the tide starts to turn
the cave falls quiet except for dripping water
salt and foam cling to your soaked and heavy boots
you sheath your blade and climb toward the light
the wrecked hull groans one final time behind you
gulls circle low as the tide finally settles
Salt spray stings as the merrow lunges.
Webbed claws rake across the wet stone.
Tide pools ripple before the attack.
A hook glints in the merrow grip.
Kelp tangles around your tired feet.
Gulls scream over the flooding cave.
The merrow slides off the slick rock.
Cold water rushes past your ankles.
Waves crash against the wrecked hull.
Its webbed hand grabs for your shield.
Sea foam hisses along the dark ledge.
You dodge the merrow sudden lunge.
Barnacles cut your palm on the climb.
The cave mouth floods with grey tide.
A rapier flashes near the tide line.
Its eyes gleam yellow in the gloom.
Kelp shields snap under its bite.
The merrow hisses and slips back.
Rock crumbles beneath your footing.
Cold spray blinds you for a moment.
The tide climbs higher every minute.
Its claws scrape along your armor.
Wet stone slows your careful step.
A bell of warning rings from shore.
The merrow circles the shallow pool.
Steam rises where the fire hits foam.
Your blade finds the gap in its scales.
The wreck groans under rising water.
Its webbed feet slap against the rock.
You brace as the wave pulls back.
The merrow snarls and bares its teeth.
Salt cracks along the cave wall.
A shield rakes across wet scales.
The tide pool churns with dark shapes.
Its grip tightens on the slick hook.
You slide past its clumsy lunge.
The cave shrieks with echoing wind.
Fire crackles against the damp kelp.
The merrow drags itself from the pool.
Your footing holds on the last ledge.
A wave slams the hull against rock.
The creature hisses through sharp teeth.
Kelp wraps around the broken oar.
You duck beneath the swinging claw.
The merrow roars from the flooded dark.
Cold fear grips you near the wreck.
Its tail slaps the churning water.
The bolt of fire lights the wet cave.
You lunge before the tide can rise.
The merrow staggers back, hissing.
Rock and foam fly from the impact.
Your rapier draws a line of blood.
The wreck creaks, ready to collapse.
A final blow sends it beneath the waves.
the sea hag waits where the tide pools flood at noon
salt cliffs crack while gulls scream over the hull
kelp ropes tangle your ankles in the flooding sea cave
her cackle echoes off the wet stone walls of the cove
a wave crashes and the hag slips back into the foam
torches hiss as the cave mouth fills with cold water
you grip the hilt while the tide rushes in fast
the wrecked hull groans under the weight of the surf
gull cries pierce the fog rolling off the salt cliffs
her claws rake the air as the tide pool ripples
you dodge the hag and the cave begins to flood
brine drips from the ceiling of the sea cave
the hag grins with rows of jagged broken teeth
kelp curls around the fallen mast in the wreck
you brace against the rock as cold spray hits
the tide climbs the steps of the flooded cave
her voice rasps like waves dragging over gravel
the old hull creaks and settles deeper in the sand
gulls wheel above the cliffs screeching a warning
you swing hard and the hag hisses in the dark
salt spray stings your eyes near the tide pool
the cave floor tilts as the water rushes higher
her shadow stretches long across the wet sand
you feel the cold current pull hard at your boots
the wrecked ship leans against the jagged rocks
kelp snaps as you wade deeper into the cave
the hag ducks behind a barnacled slab of stone
cold water floods the cave twice a day like clockwork
you hear her laugh echo through the flooded tunnel
salt crusts on the old ropes of the broken hull
the tide pool churns as something moves beneath
her fingers are long and slick with brine
you climb over slick rocks toward the cave mouth
gulls scatter as the hag rises from the foam
the cliffs drip with salt and creeping moss
water floods the cracks in the old stone floor
you brace your blade against her twisted claw
the hull sways and groans in the rising tide
her eyes glow faint green in the dark cave
you press forward as the water reaches your knees
kelp wraps the anchor chain in the sunken wreck
the hag mutters curses over the crashing waves
salt wind rattles the bones scattered on the shore
you duck under a beam in the flooded hull
the tide pool reflects a face that is not yours
her laugh rings out over the roaring surf
you brace for the wave crashing through the cave
gull feathers drift down onto the wet sand
her claws scrape stone as she climbs toward you
you slip on wet rock near the tide pool edge
the cave shakes as the tide surges once more
salt spray blinds you for a single heartbeat
her cackle fades as the water swallows the cave
you strike true and the hag shrieks in pain
The sea hag rises from the foam.
Salt cliffs shudder as the tide turns.
Kelp drags across the flooded cave floor.
Gulls scream above the wrecked hull.
The hag grins with rows of broken teeth.
Tide pools boil where the curse lands.
A cracked hull groans against the rocks.
The cave floods twice before dusk.
Barnacles cut deep into bare hands.
The hag mutters over a bowl of brine.
Waves crash through the broken hatch.
Green light flickers in the sea cave.
The hag drags her net through the surf.
Cold water rushes past your boots.
Kelp wraps tight around a rusted chain.
The tide pool hisses with foam.
A gull dives at the flooded deck.
The hag laughs from the shadows.
Salt stings the cut across your arm.
The wrecked mast tilts in the storm.
Cliffs echo with the hag cackle.
Barnacled ropes snap in the wind.
The hag slips beneath the black water.
Foam hisses along the broken planks.
A crab scuttles from the flooded hold.
The sea cave smells of rot and salt.
Waves pull the hull deeper into rocks.
The hag hums a song of drowning.
Cracked shells crunch beneath your feet.
Torches flare against the cave wall.
The tide climbs the cracked stone stair.
Seaweed chokes the sunken lantern.
The hag points a crooked finger.
Spray soaks the deck in cold salt.
A shattered mirror floats in the bilge.
The hag curse settles like fog.
Rocks grind beneath the sinking hull.
Cold brine drips from the cave roof.
The hag watches from the tide line.
Driftwood clatters against the cliff.
A wave swallows the last dry ledge.
The hag rises with a hiss of steam.
Slick stones shift beneath your grip.
The cave door groans and floods open.
Salt crust cracks along the old rope.
The hag flings brine across the deck.
Gulls scatter as the wave crashes.
The hull splits along a rusted seam.
Shadows pool where the torch gutters.
The hag counts bones on the tide line.
Sea spray stings your salted eyes.
A rope ladder sways above the surf.
The hag calls the tide to rise higher.
Cracked timbers float past the anchor.
The cave mouth swallows the last light.
the kuotoa monitor rises from the flooded tide pool
salt spray stings your eyes as the monitor lunges
a wet gill flares wide before the monitor strikes
the monitor slaps its tail across the slick rocks
kelp tangles your boots as the tide cave floods
a monitor claw rakes the wall and gouges the stone
torches hiss where the sea cave drips and echoes
the monitor circles slow through the churning surf
your blade finds the gap in its slimy hide
cold water rushes in and the monitor grins wide
the monitor drags a net of kelp across the sand
gull cries echo off the wrecked hull above
a scorching pool bubbles where the monitor waits
the monitor flicks its tongue and tastes the air
sea foam hides the monitor until it strikes fast
the cave floods twice a day and the monitor knows it
your dagger flashes and the monitor screeches back
the monitor slips behind a wall of dripping stone
a stunning croak rolls across the wet cavern
the monitor guards the gate to the flooded shrine
salt cliffs crumble as the monitor climbs higher
the monitor opens its jaws wide and hisses long
your shield takes the hit and the monitor recoils
a wave crashes and the monitor rides it toward you
the monitor drops low and sneaks along the ledge
your longsword rings against its bony skull
the monitor screams and the whole cave shakes
a flute of bone hangs from the monitors belt
the monitor plays a strange charmed little tune
your feet slip on the wet rocks near the pool
the monitor lands a hit and your vision tilts
a deep dark tunnel leads toward the monitors lair
the monitor coils and strikes with sudden speed
your aim wavers as the water churns around you
the monitor stuns you with a burst of cold song
salt cliffs echo with the monitors rasping voice
the monitor drags itself onto the wrecked hull
your blade cuts through kelp and finds its mark
the monitor twists away before the axe can land
a wet slap sounds as the monitor hits the deck
the monitor watches from behind a jagged rock
your torch flares and the monitor flinches back
the monitor swims fast through the flooded room
salt spray blinds you as the fight moves closer
the monitor claws at the hull searching for gaps
your dice roll high and the monitor takes it hard
the monitor slumps against the dripping wall
a total of eighteen damage sends it reeling
the monitor rises again from the churning water
your grip tightens as the tide rushes higher
the monitor makes a stupid grab at your ankle
the cave goes dark and the monitor vanishes fast
your last swing lands flat against its scales
the monitor howls once and the tide pool goes still
Salt spray stings your eyes near the tide pool.
Kelp ropes coil around a boot on the wet stone.
Torches hiss where the cave mouth floods twice a day.
Water churns as the monitor rises from the dark.
Barnacled claws scrape along the cracked hull.
Gulls scatter when the spear finds its mark.
Cold brine fills the tunnel behind the rocks.
Scales flash under the lantern light, then vanish.
Wet stone slips beneath careful, quiet feet.
Bubbles rise where the monitor waits, patient and still.
Bone charms rattle on the priestly staff.
Green torchlight paints the flooded chamber.
Chanting echoes off the wet cave walls.
Sea foam hides the sudden lunge of teeth.
Kelp curtains part to reveal glowing eyes.
Driftwood cracks under a heavy, webbed foot.
Salt crust flakes from ancient, pitted armor.
Tide rushes in, and the fight begins in earnest.
Slick rocks make every step a gamble.
Moonlight through the cave mouth catches wet scales.
Fins slap the surface as the creature turns.
Deep water hides more than the monitor alone.
Coral spurs line the walls like teeth.
Rope bridges sway above the flooding tide pool.
Shell fragments crunch underfoot in the dark.
Webbed hands grip a spear made of bone.
Chill fog rolls off the churning water.
Wrecked hulls groan as the tide climbs higher.
Gill slits flare, and the chant grows louder.
Stone steps vanish beneath the rising water.
Echoes of drums roll through the flooded hall.
Sharp teeth flash where the torchlight fails.
Salt cracked shields do little against claws.
Damp air smells of brine and old rot.
Slippery footing turns a dodge into a fall.
Yellow eyes track every careful step forward.
Rusted chains hang from the cave ceiling.
Sea caves swallow sound before it can carry.
Fresh scars mark the monitor stone altar.
Quick feet dodge the sweep of a scaled tail.
Pale fungus glows along the tunnel walls.
Heavy footsteps echo before the creature is seen.
Brackish water floods the lower passage fast.
Silver scales ripple as the monitor turns to strike.
Chants rise, and the water begins to churn.
Wet rope snaps under the strain of a climb.
Ancient glyphs glow faintly on the altar stone.
Foam swirls where the spear just missed.
Narrow ledges force a careful, sideways shuffle.
Distant thunder rolls above the sea cave roof.
Cracked shells litter the tide pool floor.
Spray soaks the torch, but it somehow holds.
Shadows twist as the monitor slides closer.
Calm water hides the fastest strike of all.
the chuul rises from the tide pool with claws wide
salt spray hides the pincers until they close on you
a claw finds the seam in your armor and grips hard
kelp drags across your boots as the beast climbs the rock
you roll clear before the claw can close on your arm
the sea cave floods and the chuul comes with the water
barnacles crust its shell as it drags across the stone
a bolt of light lands square on the joint of its claw
the beast holds the wrecked hull like a nest of its own
gulls scatter as the chuul rears up from the surf
your blade cuts down through the joint and it lets go
feelers whip out of the dark and search for your legs
the tide rushes in and the fight moves to higher rock
you climb the broken mast to keep clear of the claws
a claw closes on the shield and the wood begins to crack
the chuul drags a body across the sand toward its den
torches hiss as spray hits the flame near the cave mouth
you feel the poison spasm start in your gripped arm
the beast rattles its shell and turns toward the light
a longsword rings off the shell without finding a gap
the cave mouth narrows as the tide climbs your ankles
claws skid on wet stone as the chuul lunges again
you dodge right as the pincer snaps shut on empty air
the wreck groans and shifts as the beast claws through it
a wave rolls in and the fight turns slick and cold
the chuul grabs the rope and hauls itself onto the ledge
your arm goes numb where the claw briefly held it
spray and salt sting your eyes as the beast circles
a crab scuttles free as the bigger claws close in
the tide pool churns white where the chuul surges up
you find the seam in the shell and drive the blade home
the beast swings low and your legs barely clear the claw
coral crumbles under the weight of the huge shell
a bell far off rings the hour as the fight drags on
the chuul holds its ground even as the water rises
you feel the grip loosen and pull your arm free at last
salt mist rolls over the ledge hiding the next attack
the beast claws at the hull looking for a way inside
your shield takes the hit meant for your open side
the tide drags shells and driftwood past your boots
a claw grazes your side and the sting spreads fast
the chuul rises again though the blade found its mark
feelers curl around the mast searching for a grip
the cave roof drips as the fight echoes off the stone
your blade rings against shell and skips off the curve
the chuul drops back into deeper water to regroup
you climb higher as the flood closes off the low path
a claw pins the shield and nearly drags you under
the beast turns and the whole cave seems to shake
spray soaks the rope as you haul yourself up the ledge
the chuul snaps at open air where you stood a breath ago
your last strike lands deep and the grip finally fails
the tide slackens and the beast sinks back into the dark
you stand soaked and steady as the water settles again
Claws snap shut on wet stone near the tide.
Salt spray hides the chuul until it lunges.
Tentacles coil around a boot on the ledge.
Kelp drags across your ankle in the cave.
Barnacles crack as the beast heaves upward.
Gulls scatter when the shell breaks the surf.
Pincers close on driftwood, splintering it.
Foam hisses where the claw just missed you.
Tide pools ripple before the chuul surfaces.
Rusted hull creaks as claws scrape the metal.
Feelers taste the air, hunting for your scent.
Armor scrapes rock as you dodge the swipe.
Water floods the cave mouth behind the beast.
Coral edges cut your palm as you climb free.
Shadows pool where the tide has not reached.
Spray blinds you just as the claw swings wide.
Rope snaps taut against the slick sea cave wall.
Echoes bounce off wet stone as claws click.
Mast timbers groan under the crawling weight.
Chuul rises from the pool, dripping and huge.
Bell of the wreck clangs in the churning surf.
Sand shifts under heavy legs near the shore.
Torchlight flickers on wet, jointed limbs.
Grip tightens on the blade as claws approach.
Wave crashes, hiding the creak of shifting rock.
Slime trails mark where the beast dragged past.
Cliffside crumbles as claws dig for purchase.
Breath fogs in the cold, salty cave air.
Chuul snaps its claw just short of your arm.
Driftwood splinters under a sudden pincer blow.
Moonlight catches the shell before it moves.
Barnacled claws rake across the cave floor.
Current pulls at your legs near the flooded ledge.
Seaweed hides the shape until it strikes.
Ledge crumbles as the beast lunges forward.
Steam rises where warm blood meets cold spray.
Echo of dripping water masks its slow approach.
Chuul grips the rock, feelers testing the wind.
Blade rings against a claw of solid shell.
Fog rolls in low across the flooding cave.
Timber snaps as the chuul forces the gap wider.
Grit and salt sting your eyes mid swing.
Anchor chain rattles as the tide keeps rising.
Pincers scissor shut where your foot just was.
Cave mouth narrows just as the claws reach in.
Wet stone slips beneath your boots mid step.
Chuul drags itself over the wrecked hull plating.
Spear jabs at the joint between claw and arm.
Reef edges glint as the beast circles closer.
Bubbles rise where the chuul waits, submerged.
Sudden swell lifts the beast toward the ledge.
Grip slips on kelp as claws snap shut again.
Cavern shakes as heavy claws test the walls.
Salt crust flakes off the shell with each blow.
Tide retreats, leaving the chuul stranded and mad.
salt spray stings your eyes as the trident swings low
kelp ropes tangle your ankles - the baron laughs from the tide
a crown of barnacles glints; the baron wades from the surf
waves crash against the hull - your blade finds the gap
the baron's trident cracks the deck plank by plank
gull-cries scatter as the tide pool churns red
you dodge left; the trident's tines rake the rail
a wet slap of scales - the baron slides toward you
torches flare against the sea-cave's dripping walls
you brace on the wrecked hull as the tide surges in
the baron's crown catches the moonlight - and your eye
salt spray blinds you for a heartbeat too long
a barnacled fist swings; you duck beneath its arc
the cave floods twice a day - and it's rising now
you feel the trident's cold shadow pass your shoulder
the baron roars; kelp strands whip across your face
your boots slip on wet stone - steady your footing
a jab from the trident grazes your forearm badly
the baron circles - always the same patient stance
you parry with a shriek of metal on scale
the tide pool boils where the baron last stood
salt-crusted armor gleams under the flickering torch
you swing wide; the baron's laugh echoes off wet rock
a wave slams the hull and knocks your footing loose
the baron's trident finds the gap in your guard
you steady your grip as the sea-cave groans overhead
claws rake the deck - the baron closes in fast
the crown tilts as the baron sizes up your stance
you feel the cold tide climb past your knees
a hiss of breath - the baron's gills flare wide
you dive past the trident's arc and strike true
the baron's collar of shells clatters with each step
spray and foam mask the baron's next move
you plant your feet on the tilting, wet deck
the trident whistles past your ear - too close
a low growl rises from beneath the flooded cave
you catch the baron's wrist and twist the blade free
the tide drags at your legs as the baron advances
scales flash silver in the dim sea-cave light
you brace against the hull as the baron lunges
the baron's crown slips - and you seize the moment
a crack of thunder rolls over the salt cliffs
you feel the deck shift beneath the baron's weight
the trident's tip drags a line across wet planks
you sidestep as the baron's tail sweeps the floor
the sea-cave echoes with the baron's rasping laugh
a splash - the baron vanishes into churning water
you brace for the strike that never quite lands
the baron surges up through the flooding tide pool
salt stings the cut along your knuckles
you swing your blade in a wide, desperate arc
the baron's trident locks against your guard
you feel the tide turn cold around your boots
the crown of barnacles cracks under your strike
you stand your ground as the last wave crashes through
